title: anthriscus cerefolium var. longirostris
intro: Anthriscus cerefolium var. longirostris, commonly known as chervil, is a perennial herb belonging to the parsley family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of chervil include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Chervil can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as used as a garnish or added to salads.
